verse_text: a woman came to Him having an alabaster flask of very costly fragrant oil, and she poured it on His head as He sat at the table.

links.context: The other disciples said the same thing Judas said (v. 5), but with a very different motive (see [[MAT.26:6]]-13 and note). Judas spoke from greed, while the others spoke from the virtue of charity. That Jesus put a thief in charge of the money shows that by every means He attempted to save Judas: He fulfilled Judas' lust for money; He allowed Judas to exercise apostolic authority (6:11; see also [[MRK.6:7]]); He washed Judas' feet with the other disciples (13:5); and He allowed him to partake at the table of the Mystical Supper (13:26). Yet Judas could never overcome his greed (see note at [[MAT.26:14]]-16).

symbolic.title: Returning to the Garden of Eden

links.context: This prophetic verse was fulfilled by Mary of Bethany's anointing of the Lord with spikenard for His burial while He was at the table ([[MAT.26:6]], [[MAT.26:7]]; [[MRK.14:3]]; [[LUK.7:37]], [[LUK.7:38]]; Jn 12:3).

symbolic.title: Returning to the Garden of Eden
